documents.title: Guidelines for Use of AI at UF
Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence policy

ai_strategy.summary: UF is a national benchmark for institution-wide AI transformation. Since 2020, it has pursued AI Across the Curriculum, 100+ AI faculty hires, HiPerGator/NVIDIA infrastructure, NaviGator AI, AI Fundamentals and discipline certificates, guidelines for AI use, and formal responsible-use policy.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: University of Florida's AI policy posture is best characterized as comprehensive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

bodies.scope: NaviGator AI and HiPerGator AI access.

governance_structure.summary: University of Florida's public AI governance is organized through AI at UF / AI2 Center, UF Information Technology, Office of the Provost.

initiatives.title: AI Across the Curriculum
initiatives.description: UF's initiative infuses AI learning broadly across all majors, created a university-wide AI Fundamentals certificate and discipline-specific certificates, and aims for every student to graduate AI-literate.
initiatives.significance: University of Florida's ai across the curriculum is a signal of how an R1 institution is adapting AI across policy, teaching, research, operations, or workforce needs.

investment.description: Publicly disclosed amount associated with AI Across the Curriculum.

initiatives.policy_implications: Supports responsible AI adoption through human oversight, privacy controls, disclosure, or course-level expectations.

initiatives.title: NaviGator AI
initiatives.description: UFIT released NaviGator AI, described by UF as higher education's first comprehensive self-service AI platform, hosting 26 LLMs from providers including OpenAI, Google, Stability AI, Anthropic, Meta, Mistral, and NVIDIA NIMs.
initiatives.significance: University of Florida's navigator ai is a signal of how an R1 institution is adapting AI across policy, teaching, research, operations, or workforce needs.

investment.description: No specific dollar amount disclosed in consulted public sources.

initiatives.policy_implications: Supports responsible AI adoption through human oversight, privacy controls, disclosure, or course-level expectations.

initiatives.title: HiPerGator AI infrastructure
initiatives.description: HiPerGator powers UF's AI efforts with 1,120 NVIDIA A100 GPUs; UF also describes planned investments in DGX B200/Blackwell and storage expansions.
initiatives.significance: University of Florida's hipergator ai infrastructure is a signal of how an R1 institution is adapting AI across policy, teaching, research, operations, or workforce needs.

investment.description: Publicly disclosed amount associated with HiPerGator AI infrastructure.

initiatives.title: Guidelines and responsible-use policy
initiatives.description: UF publishes Guidelines for Use of AI and a Responsible Use of AI policy that emphasize accessibility, transparency, security, human-in-the-loop decisions, privacy, intellectual property, and academic integrity.

initiatives.title: AI faculty hiring and strategic investments
initiatives.description: UF states it launched a two-year AI hiring initiative that brought 106 AI faculty members across colleges and distributed nearly $19M to fund AI efforts in 10 colleges.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: AI is for Everyone
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UF guidelines make broad AI access, ethics, transparency, accessibility, bias mitigation, and human values explicit principles.

regulatory_context.summary: University of Florida operates in Florida and within State University System of Florida; public AI guidance should be read alongside applicable state, federal, and system privacy, security, accessibility, and academic-integrity requirements.

narrative_summary: University of Florida (Gainesville, FL) is an R1 institution whose public AI posture shows activity across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tooling, workforce readiness, and access. UF is a national benchmark for institution-wide AI transformation. Since 2020, it has pursued AI Across the Curriculum, 100+ AI faculty hires, HiPerGator/NVIDIA infrastructure, NaviGator AI, AI Fundamentals and discipline certificates, guidelines for AI use, and formal responsible-use policy. The most concrete public initiatives captured here are AI Across the Curriculum; NaviGator AI; HiPerGator AI infrastructure; Guidelines and responsible-use policy; AI faculty hiring and strategic investments. Across the profile, the strongest evidence comes from official university AI hubs, policy pages, teaching resources, research-center pages, and program pages rather than secondary reporting. The institution_type is set to r1_research from the canonical Carnegie R1 list. Known gaps are intentionally explicit: leadership names, user counts, detailed budgets, and compute counts are only included when they surfaced in public sources. Overall, the profile supports at least five AI initiatives and evidences strategy/leadership, governance, teaching/curriculum, research infrastructure, student services or enterprise tooling, workforce/community, partnerships, and equity dimensions where public sources permit.
